title: What to know about the ‘massive’ military bunker beneath Trump’s ballroom
author: New York Times Service
bullets: Work crews are constructing a large underground military complex beneath Trump's planned $400 million White House ballroom.
The project includes bomb shelters, medical facilities, and secure communication methods, linked to enhanced White House security.
A judge halted the project pending congressional approval, despite Secret Service claims it is crucial for safety.

title: Just one Mass. college ranks among the nation’s best graduate schools, per U.S. News
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: MIT ranked No. 1 for best graduate engineering program in U.S. News 2026 rankings.
Massachusetts medical schools did not reach top spots in research or primary care categories.
Boston College's master’s in nursing program ranked highest in Massachusetts, reaching 26th nationally.

title: Suffolk DA Kevin Hayden appears to have a new primary challenger. She used to work in his office.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Linda Champion, former member of Kevin Hayden's team, filed paperwork for a potential Suffolk DA campaign.
Champion has previously run for Suffolk DA, finishing fifth in the 2018 Democratic primary.
Hayden faces scrutiny over charges against a Boston officer, amidst his reelection campaign preparations.

title: Malden cook goes viral for selling trendy Ilocos empanadas out of her apartment
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Janz Van, a Malden cook, went viral selling Ilocos empanadas from her apartment, gaining widespread social media attention.
Malden's Board of Health shut down Van's operations due to zoning laws prohibiting residential kitchen permits.
Van is raising funds for a food truck and plans to sell her empanadas at pop-ups and on the road in New England.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo paid high praise to Celtics’ coach Joe Mazzulla
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla for instilling a winning mentality despite challenges.
Boston Celtics hold the second-best odds for an NBA title despite Jayson Tatum's injury and roster changes.
Milwaukee Bucks, led by Doc Rivers, finished 31-47, missing playoffs amid Antetokounmpo's future uncertainty.

title: Person seriously injured in fall at Firefighting Academy facility
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A person was seriously injured in a fall at the Firefighting Academy in Stow on Tuesday morning.
Stow Fire Department responded at 9:05 a.m., providing medical care and requesting extra ambulance support.
The injured person was taken by ground to UMass Memorial Medical Center due to unsuitable weather for air transport.

title: TravelMag went on a seafood road trip in Maine and named these ‘can’t-miss’ restaurants. Which is your favorite?
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: TravelMag highlighted 10 'can't-miss' seafood restaurants from a road trip along the Maine coast.
Top mentions include The Clam Shack in Kennebunkport and Bagaduce Lunch in Penobscot for their legendary dishes.
Boston.com invites readers to share their favorite restaurant picks from the TravelMag tour for a future feature.

title: Ex-Stoughton cop Matthew Farwell’s DNA was on strap used to strangle Sandra Birchmore, prosecutors say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: DNA evidence links former Stoughton officer Matthew Farwell to the strap used in Sandra Birchmore's 2021 death, say prosecutors.
Farwell faces charges for allegedly killing Birchmore, staging her death as a suicide, and exploiting her since she was underage.
Despite believing he was the father, DNA evidence excludes Farwell as the father of Birchmore's unborn child at her death.

title: A young mother rescued her 2-year-old son after their car crashed into a Vermont river
author: John Waller
bullets: A 26-year-old mother rescued her 2-year-old son after their car crashed into the Ottauquechee River in Vermont.
Hartford firefighters responded quickly, finding the pair safe on the riverbank after escaping the submerged vehicle.
The mother and boy were taken to Dartmouth Hitchcock Medical Center for evaluations; the crash cause is still under investigation.

title: All the places you can go on the Amtrak Downeaster
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Amtrak Downeaster runs a 143-mile route from Boston to Brunswick, Maine, with stops in 12 communities across MA, NH, and ME.
Passengers enjoy reclining seats, free Wi-Fi, and on-board food options. The train is wheelchair accessible and allows small pets and bicycles.
Downeaster offers fares ranging $3 to $38. Seniors get 50% off. Late-night returns available for events without departure delays.
